segments.text: Why is there a red dot inside this bolt?
I honestly thought it was just a sticker
or some cheap paint. But I was totally
wrong. This is actually a tiny working
mechanical screen hiding in plain sight.
See, normally when you build huge
machines and bridges, it's kind of a
guessing game. You can look at a regular
bolt, but you have absolutely no idea if
it's actually tight or safe. The danger
is completely invisible and you
literally have to use heavy tools to
check every single one. So, someone came
up with this brilliant idea of a smart
bolt. They built a tiny coloranging
window straight into the metal. Here is
how it works. As you tighten it, the
bolt actually stretches. And that
stretch changes how the light hits the
inside of the window, turning it from
bright red to pitch dark. Red means it's
loose, but the black mean it's tight.
Now imagine a giant machine with
thousand of these bolts. You don't need
to check them anymore. Just walk by and
use your eyes. If you don't see any red,
you know it is safe. Simple, but so
cool. Subscribe for more.
